name: vit-adapter
description: Adapt pretrained Vision Transformers to new tasks using lightweight task-specific adapters, reducing fine-tuning parameters by 90% while maintaining accuracy.
keywords: vision transformer, adapter, transfer learning, parameter efficiency
content:
## ViT Task-Specific Adapters

### Core Concept

Task-specific adapters insert lightweight layers into frozen ViT models, enabling efficient transfer to new visual tasks. Rather than fine-tuning all parameters, adapters learn task-specific transformations using only a small fraction of parameters.

### Implementation

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

class ViTAdapter(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, hidden_dim=768, adapter_dim=64):
        super().__init__()
        # Down-project, activate, up-project
        self.down = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, adapter_dim)
        self.activation = nn.GELU()
        self.up = nn.Linear(adapter_dim, hidden_dim)
        self.ln = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)

    def forward(self, x):
        # Residual connection
        return x + self.up(self.activation(self.down(self.ln(x))))

class AdaptedViT(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, base_vit, adapter_dim=64, num_tasks=1):
        super().__init__()
        self.vit = base_vit

        # Freeze base model
        for param in self.vit.parameters():
            param.requires_grad = False

        # Add adapters to each layer
        self.adapters = nn.ModuleList([
            ViTAdapter(768, adapter_dim)
            for _ in range(len(base_vit.encoder.layer))
        ])

    def forward(self, pixel_values):
        # Get embeddings
        outputs = self.vit(pixel_values, output_hidden_states=True)

        # Apply adapters
        adapted = outputs.last_hidden_state
        for adapter in self.adapters:
            adapted = adapter(adapted)

        return adapted
```

### When to Use
- Pretrained ViT available, need task adaptation
- Parameter budget limited
- Want to avoid catastrophic forgetting
- Multiple tasks from single base model

### When NOT to Use
- Base model domain mismatch
- Insufficient adapter capacity needed
- Fine-tuning parameters not bottleneck

---
